Tommaso Bosi

Via Diana 35 · Rome, Italy · Tommaso.bosi@uniroma3.it

I am a Ph.D. student in Computer Science and Automation with expertise in the algorithmic development cycle for transport companies. Proficient in programming languages such as Python, C, Java, and SQL. With strong project and business management skills, I also have experience in sustainable mobility projects.

Experience

University Teaching

Roma Tre University

Supplementary teaching and tutoring in Geometry and Linear Algebra.

September 2022 - Present

Roma Tre University

Ph.D. student representative of the Engineering Department.

February 2022 - Present

Member of the EWG SSC

EURO - The Association of European Operational Research Societies

Member of the EWG SSC, EURO working group on Sustainable Supply Chains, and AIRO, Associazione Italiana Ricerca Operativa.

August 2022 - Present

Research Trainee

CFL - Société Nationale des Chemins de Fer Luxembourgeois

Development and analysis of wagon shunting policies for shunting yard operating full train load services.

September 2021 - January 2022

Research Trainee

Trenitalia

Development of algorithms for the on-line train calendars generation of passenger trains.

September 2019 - April 2020

Publications

Apart from being a web developer, I enjoy most of my time being outdoors. In the winter, I am an avid skier and novice ice climber. During the warmer months here in Colorado, I enjoy mountain biking, free climbing, and kayaking.

When forced indoors, I follow a number of sci-fi and fantasy genre movies and television shows, I am an aspiring chef, and I spend a large amount of my free time exploring the latest technology advancements in the front-end web development world.
